name: Forty Martyrs Meatballs
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the lamb with the garlic, salt to taste, pepper, mint, pine nuts, and parsley. Mix thoroughly.
2. Form into 40 meatballs about 1 1/4 inches in diameter (divide the meat into 4 parts, then each part into 10 meatballs).
3. Heat the oil in a large skillet.
4. Sauté the meatballs until nicely browned on the outside, but still slightly pink inside. Turn them often with a spatula.
5. Remove excess grease as it is rendered.
6. Serve the meatballs on a bed of lettuce arranged in 5 rows of 8 each or another clearly numerical pattern.
7. Accompany with Rice or Bulghur Pilaf.
8. Variation: You can add 1/2 teaspoon allspice or coriander to the mixture.
In many countries, a special dish is prepared annually to honor the memory of the Forty Martyrs of Sebaste...
In Aremenia, forty stuffed wheat balls (ingredients difficult to find) are eaten; in Greece, dishes featuring 40 herbs or layers are also honored.
